steps.title: Factor the coefficient and the variable part
steps.content:
$63 = 7 \times 9$ and $x^2 = x \cdot x$, so:
$$63x^2 = (7x)(9x)$$
Verify: $7 \cdot 9 = 63$ and $x \cdot x = x^2$. ✓

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$63(1+x^2)$': Expanding gives $63 + 63x^2 e 63x^2$. The extra $63$ is not present in the original. ✗
  • Choice B — '$(7x)(9x)$': $7 imes 9 = 63$ and $x imes x = x^2$, so $(7x)(9x) = 63x^2$. ✓
  • Choice C — '$(7x^2)(9x^2)$': $7 imes 9 = 63$ but $x^2 imes x^2 = x^4 e x^2$. Wrong degree. ✗
  • Choice D — '$(63x^2)(x)$': $63x^2 imes x = 63x^3 e 63x^2$. Wrong degree. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
